intro: Tupacamaria lagoensis, also known as Laguiole tree or Tupacamaria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Tupacamaria lagoensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
